questions.question: Articles that are intended for delivery from the post office to which they are sent are known as?
questions.explanation: Station articles are postal articles intended for delivery from the post office or sorting office to which they are directed, as opposed to sorting articles which are meant for onward transmission.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Bag which is never dispatched loose?
questions.explanation: Due to security reasons and the high value of contents, an Insured bag is never dispatched loose; it is always enclosed within a designated secure bag or mail bag.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which bags are due bags?
questions.explanation: Due bags are those which must be dispatched regularly, whether there are any contents to send or not. Both Account bags and Transit bags fall under this category.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which statement is correct regarding Post Office Saving Accounts?

options.A: Only one account can be opened by an individual.
options.B: All deposits / withdrawals shall be made in whole rupees only.

questions.explanation: All the given statements are correct rules governing the Post Office Savings Account (POSA).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Maximum how much amount can be deposited in a single Monthly Income Scheme Account?
questions.explanation: Following recent revisions, the maximum investment limit for a single account under the Post Office Monthly Income Scheme (POMIS) is Rs. 9 lakh.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Post Offices, which are not being closed on Sunday and Postal Holidays, are known as?
questions.explanation: Night Post Offices operate with extended hours on regular working days and remain open for specific transactions even on Sundays and postal holidays.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The impression of frank should consist ___ dies.
questions.explanation: A valid franking machine impression must consist of both a Value die (indicating the postage amount) and a License die (identifying the user's license details).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Articles which can be posted in letter boxes are:
questions.explanation: Inland Letter cards can be dropped in letter boxes. Machine franked articles must be handed over at the post office counter and are strictly prohibited from being dropped in letter boxes.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Fleet Mail Office is situated in:
questions.explanation: The Fleet Mail Office (FMO), which serves the postal needs of the Indian Navy, is located in Mumbai.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Articles which can be delivered through post boxes, are:
questions.explanation: All fully prepaid, unregistered articles like inland letter cards, postcards, and registered newspapers can be delivered through a rented Post Box.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: For availing facility of post box, a person or firm will have to submit the application to:
questions.explanation: An application to rent a Post Box must be submitted in the prescribed format to the Postmaster of the concerned post office.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which statement is correct regarding Returned Letter Office (RLO)?

options.A: In RLO, further efforts are made to find the addresses of articles.
options.B: Refused articles are treated in the RLO, in the same manner as articles the addresses of which cannot be found.

questions.explanation: The Returned Letter Office (RLO) makes extensive efforts to locate the addressee or sender for undeliverable mail. Refused articles and articles with untraceable addresses are both processed by the RLO to find the sender.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Second Class Mail includes:
questions.explanation: Second Class Mails include Book Packets, Blind Literature Packets, and Registered Newspapers. They do not get the free air transmission given to First Class Mail.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Philately consists:
questions.explanation: Philately (the collection and study of postage stamps) encompasses items like First Day Covers, Special Covers, Information Sheets, and commemorative stamps.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Where foreigners can avail foreign philatelic deposit service?
questions.explanation: Foreign philatelic deposit accounts for overseas collectors and customers are uniquely maintained at the Philatelic Bureau in Mumbai.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: When was the Postal Life Insurance introduced?
questions.explanation: Postal Life Insurance (PLI), the oldest life insurer in India, was established on 1st February 1884 as a welfare scheme for postal employees.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Whole Life Assurance is also known as?
questions.explanation: Under the Postal Life Insurance (PLI) scheme, the Whole Life Assurance policy is officially branded as 'Suraksha'.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Minimum sum assured for Gram Suraksha scheme is:
questions.explanation: For the Rural Postal Life Insurance (RPLI) Whole Life Assurance policy known as 'Gram Suraksha', the minimum sum assured is Rs. 10,000.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: 'Postal Articles' include:
questions.explanation: The term 'Postal Articles' is a broad category encompassing letters, postcards, sample packets, book packets, parcels, and more.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The Officer in-charge of a set of a transit section is called a:
questions.explanation: The official in charge of a set of a transit section in the Railway Mail Service is designated as a Mail Guard or Mail Agent, depending on their grade and the size of the section.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Bags which are not due bags and returned empty to UBO/DBO/CBO / PO or sacks containing such bags are treated as:
questions.explanation: According to the Postal Manual Volume V, surplus empty bags returned to bag offices are generally enclosed and treated/labeled as Packet Bags.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: For which address value payable articles and money orders will not be booked?
questions.explanation: Value Payable (VP) articles and Money Orders are not accepted for booking if addressed to personnel at an Army Post Office (APO).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: For how many years identification cards which issued by the post office to tourists will be valid?
questions.explanation: Postal Identity Cards issued by the Post Office as a means of establishing identity for postal transactions are valid for three years.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which statement is correct during delivery of postal article addressed to deceased person:

options.A: Articles are ordinarily dealt with in the same manner as unclaimed articles.
options.B: Postmasters are authorized to exercise their discretion in delivering such articles to any near relative of the deceased.
options.C: The applicant will have to address the application to the Head of the Circle concerned.

questions.explanation: All stated rules apply: unclaimed treatment is standard, postmasters have discretionary power for near relatives (for un-insured/unregistered mail), and specific formal applications can be directed to the Head of the Circle.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Maximum weight of a letter may be?
questions.explanation: The maximum permissible weight for a domestic letter in the Indian postal system is 2 kilograms.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Who can open Mahila Samman Saving Certificate?

options.B: By the guardian on behalf of a minor girl

questions.explanation: The Mahila Samman Savings Certificate can be opened by a woman for herself, or by a guardian on behalf of a minor girl child.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: This office is used to reduce the number of loose bags?
questions.explanation: A Transit Office helps reduce the handling of individual loose bags by consolidating multiple bags meant for the same onward destination into a single Transit Bag.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Sorting of letters is not done in this unit of RMS.
questions.explanation: A Transit Mail Office (TMO) exclusively handles closed bags. It receives and dispatches them without opening them to sort individual letters.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which offices cannot be unit bag offices?
questions.explanation: Unit Bag Offices (UBO) are typically Head Offices or Sub-Offices. Branch Post Offices (BO) and Extra Departmental Sub Offices (EDSO) are generally tied to their account offices and do not function as independent UBOs.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which unit works as a District Bag Office?
questions.explanation: In the postal bag accounting structure, the Head Record Office (HRO) of the RMS division generally functions as the District Bag Office (DBO).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following articles of Indian Constitution cover the provisions related to citizenship?
questions.explanation: Part II of the Constitution of India (Articles 5 to 11) deals with the Citizenship of India.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: The Ranthambhore National Park is famous for?
questions.explanation: Ranthambhore National Park in Rajasthan is one of the largest and most famous national parks in Northern India, widely known for its Bengal tiger population.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Famous Indus Valley civilization related Dholavira site is situated in:
questions.explanation: Dholavira, an archaeological site showcasing a major city of the ancient Indus Valley Civilization, is located in the Kutch district of Gujarat.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: With which of the following is related CITES in context with the global issues?

options.C: Poverty alleviation programme of United Nations

questions.explanation: CITES stands for the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora. It is a major multilateral treaty focused on environmental protection and wildlife conservation.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Taxila was the capital of which ancient Mahajanpada?
questions.explanation: In ancient India, the renowned city of Taxila (Takshashila) served as the capital of the Gandhara Mahajanapada.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Which ethical principle emphasizes fairness and impartiality?
questions.explanation: In ethics, the principle of 'Justice' fundamentally emphasizes fairness, equality, and acting with impartiality in interactions and distributions.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Gangaur festival is celebrated in?
questions.explanation: Gangaur is a major and vibrant festival traditionally celebrated by women primarily in the Indian state of Rajasthan.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Who is known as 'Father of Indian Constitution'?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly and is highly revered as the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: Who is known as 'Bismarck of India'?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel is known as the 'Bismarck of India' for his decisive role in the political integration of India's princely states post-independence.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Where is famous 'Kutub Minar' situated?
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar (Kutub Minar), a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is situated in the Mehrauli area of New Delhi.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Calculate the simple interest on Rs. 8000 for 15 months at a rate of 6 paise per rupee per month?
questions.explanation: Rate = 6 paise per rupee per month = 6% per month. Time = 15 months. Simple Interest = Principal × Rate × Time / 100 = 8000 × 6 × 15 / 100 = 80 × 90 = Rs. 7200.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: What is the average of the first six multiples of 4?
questions.explanation: The first six multiples of 4 are 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24. Average = (First term + Last term) / 2 = (4 + 24) / 2 = 28 / 2 = 14.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Devi completed the school project in 20 days. How many days will Aruna take to complete the same work if she is 25% more efficient than Devi?
questions.explanation: Efficiency of Aruna = 125% of Devi. Ratio of efficiency (Devi : Aruna) = 100 : 125 = 4 : 5. Time taken is inversely proportional to efficiency, so ratio of time = 5 : 4. If 5 parts = 20 days, 1 part = 4 days. Therefore, Aruna takes 4 × 4 = 16 days.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: While going to office, Asha travels at a speed of 30 Km/hour and on her way back, she travels at a speed of 45 Km/hour. What is the average speed of whole journey?
questions.explanation: When distances are equal, Average Speed = (2 × S1 × S2) / (S1 + S2). Average speed = (2 × 30 × 45) / (30 + 45) = 2700 / 75 = 36 Km/hour.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: If the price of 5 kg wheat is Rs. 150. Find the value of 24 kg wheat?
questions.explanation: Cost of 5 kg = Rs. 150. Cost of 1 kg = 150 / 5 = Rs. 30. Therefore, cost of 24 kg = 24 × 30 = Rs. 720.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Evaluate: 15 + 3² ÷ 3 × 2 - 5 = ?
questions.explanation: Applying BODMAS rules: First, evaluate the exponent: 3² = 9. Equation becomes 15 + 9 ÷ 3 × 2 - 5. Next, perform division: 9 ÷ 3 = 3. Equation becomes 15 + 3 × 2 - 5. Perform multiplication: 3 × 2 = 6. Finally, addition and subtraction: 15 + 6 - 5 = 21 - 5 = 16. (Note: Adapted to match the answer key based on a typographical error in the original exam text).
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Evaluate: 5 × (2 × 3⁴) ÷ 6 + 7 - 8 = ?
questions.explanation: Using BODMAS: First exponent: 3⁴ = 81. Next solve brackets: (2 × 81) = 162. Equation becomes: 5 × 162 ÷ 6 + 7 - 8. Division: 162 ÷ 6 = 27. Multiplication: 5 × 27 = 135. Finally: 135 + 7 - 8 = 134.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: A fruit seller had some apples. He sells 40% apples and still has 420 apples. What is the total number of apples he had originally?
questions.explanation: If the seller sells 40%, the remaining apples constitute 60% of the total. Let the total apples be x. So, 60% of x = 420 => (60/100) × x = 420 => x = 420 × 100 / 60 = 700.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: If an article is sold for Rs. 178 at a loss of 11%, what should be its selling price in order to earn a profit of 11%?
questions.explanation: Selling Price (SP) = Rs. 178 at 11% loss. So, 89% of Cost Price (CP) = 178. CP = 178 / 0.89 = Rs. 200. To earn a profit of 11%, the new SP should be 111% of CP = 200 × 1.11 = Rs. 222.00.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability
